message:
refactor(http): consolidate header parsing back to str-first flow

Reverts the byte-first header parsing direction introduced earlier and
consolidates on a str-first approach:

- parse_header_line_bytes() now validates UTF-8 upfront via
  std::str::from_utf8 and delegates to parse_header_line(&str).  This
  is simpler and catches invalid UTF-8 at the boundary before any
  field-name/value inspection.
- parse_header_line() works entirely with &str slices — field-name
  whitespace and tchar validation use the existing is_valid_header_name
  (which operates on str), and value extraction uses str slicing instead
  of byte-index gymnastics with separate from_utf8 calls.
- decode_head() converts the full head block to &str once upfront and
  uses an index-based cursor (usize offset) instead of a shrinking byte
  slice.  This avoids repeated &str↔&[u8] conversions and makes the
  parsing loop easier to follow.

The net effect is fewer code paths, one less helper function
(is_valid_header_name_bytes removed since is_valid_header_name suffices),
and the same correctness guarantees (invalid UTF-8 is caught at entry).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
